sample_details.model_response: B — I would keep the stall open late because the additional revenue generated by evening customers outweighs the opportunity cost of missing early sales and reinforces the family's work ethic.

Option B: You keep the stall open — customers who come later spend more freely, every sale you miss goes to a competitor, your family understands that providing for them means working when the work is available, and the evening hours are the most profitable.

What do you do?

Option B: You extend the visit — one more day harms nothing, your mother's tears are genuine, rigid adherence to schedules over human connection is not virtue but coldness, and the convent will manage without you.
